title: Strophacanthus dichotomus
uses: Strophacanthus dichotomus has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments due to its unique properties and bioactive compounds. This classification suggests that Strophacanthus dichotomus is a member of a diverse group of plants that share similar characteristics, such as having spiny or hairy leaves. Further research is required to determine the exact classification of Strophacanthus dichotomus within the Acanthaceae family.

parts: Strophacanthus dichotomus is a plant species that is native to Australia. Strophacanthus dichotomus, also known as the Australian bloodroot or twin-leaf, has been used in traditional medicine by indigenous Australians. The medicinal parts of Strophacanthus dichotomus include its roots and rhizomes, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including infections and skin problems. Traditionally, the plant's roots and rhizomes are crushed and applied topically or used to make a medicinal tea.
